articleTitle: Sleep quality and diabetic neuropathy: A review and exploratory meta-analysis.
takeaways: People with diabetic peripheral neuropathy, particularly those experiencing nerve pain, have significantly more sleep disturbances compared to those without neuropathy
Gabapentinoid medications (such as gabapentin and pregabalin) reduced sleep interference scores by an average of 1.07 points in people with painful diabetic peripheral neuropathy
Individuals with diabetic autonomic neuropathy show high rates of obstructive sleep apnea, suggesting the need for sleep apnea screening in this population

Poor sleep quality in diabetes may actually increase the risk of developing diabetic neuropathy, creating a potential cycle where nerve damage worsens sleep and poor sleep promotes further nerve damage
